The Tang dynasty was a period in Chinese history ruled under the house of Li, lasting from 618 to 907 AD. It is often considered the high point of classical Chinese civilization. During this era, China expanded its borders significantly, established the Grand Canal, experienced a cultural boom characterized by poetry (such as that of Li Bai and Du Fu), and engaged extensively with foreign cultures through the Silk Road.
